title: [code-review] safe egraph select rewrites skip predicate validation
description:
Review evidence from the last-six-hours safe-egraph hardening:
- safe_algebraic_rules still includes select-true, select-false, and select-same in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.
- fj-lax eval_select validates the condition and rejects complex conditions with 'select condition must be boolean, got complex dtype' in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs. It also validates scalar/tensor kind and shape compatibility across the condition and branch values.
- select-same rewrites select(c, a, a) directly to a. With a complex condition, the original program fails closed, while the safe/default optimized program can return a without evaluating the invalid predicate.
- select-true/select-false have the same validation-skipping shape/kind risk for malformed branch inputs, because the original select checks matching scalar/tensor kinds and branch dtype promotion even when the selected predicate is a literal.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph regression that builds out = Select(cond, x, x) with invars [cond, x].
2. Evaluate the original with args [Value::scalar_complex128(1.0, 0.0), Value::scalar_f64(7.0)]. Expected: eval_jaxpr returns the existing fj-lax TypeMismatch for complex select condition.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe()) and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with the same args. Current safe/default can apply select-same and return Value::scalar_f64(7.0), converting a fail-closed input error into success.
4. Add a literal-condition malformed-branch case such as Select(Literal::I64(1), scalar, tensor) to cover select-true/false skipping matching-kind validation.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_select_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph select_validation -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: remove select-same/select-true/select-false from safe mode unless the egraph carries enough type/shape/predicate validity evidence, or keep them only in a typed lowering phase after validation has already been proven.
close_reason: Moved validation-skipping select rewrites out of safe/default egraph rules and added select validation regressions

title: Implement U32/U64 unary arithmetic in eval_unary_int_or_float (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Handle Literal::U32 and Literal::U64 in eval_unary_int_or_float() at crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:942-945.
WHERE: The match arm at line 942 returns "unsigned unary arithmetic not yet implemented" for both U32 and U64.
HOW: For each unary op that calls this function:
  - neg: U32/U64 negation is wrapping in JAX (twos complement). Implement as wrapping_neg() or cast to i64, negate, cast back. Check JAX oracle behavior.
  - abs: Identity for unsigned types (always non-negative). Return input unchanged.
  - sign: 0 for 0, 1 for any positive value. Trivial for unsigned.
  - Other unary ops routed here should be checked case-by-case.
Check which callers use eval_unary_int_or_float: grep for it and ensure each caller passes appropriate uint_op alongside int_op and float_op. May need to add a uint_op parameter or handle U32/U64 separately in the match.
WHY: U32/U64 are valid JAX dtypes. Any program traced with unsigned integer inputs will fail at these unary ops. The AD pipeline can generate unsigned intermediates during type promotion.
TESTING: Test neg(U32(5)), abs(U32(5)), sign(U32(0)), sign(U32(7)). Test neg(U64(1)). Verify against JAX oracle if available.
ACCEPTANCE: The string "unsigned unary arithmetic not yet implemented" no longer exists in the codebase.

title: fj-ad RFFT VJP widens F32 input gradient to F64 via take_real_part + F64 scale literal
description: Same dtype-widening pattern as frankenjax-4y5q but in RFFT VJP (the symmetric counterpart). crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2622-2645 RFFT VJP zero-pads the cotangent, runs Ifft, scales by 'Literal::from_f64(fft_length as f64)', then calls take_real_part. Two issues: (1) the F64 scale literal promotes a Complex64 Ifft result to Complex128; (2) take_real_part is hardcoded to F64 output regardless of input dtype, so an F32 RFFT input yields an F64 gradient. Acceptance: (a) make the scale literal match the cotangent's dtype via the existing reciprocal_scalar_matching_cotangent_dtype helper (or a sibling that returns n rather than 1/n); (b) parameterize take_real_part with an explicit output dtype so the caller can request F32 when appropriate; (c) RFFT VJP should pass DType::F32 when the cotangent was BF16/F16/F32 and DType::F64 otherwise; (d) add a regression test asserting RFFT VJP for an F32 input + Complex64 cotangent produces an F32 gradient (not F64); (e) confirm existing F64 RFFT VJP path still passes; (f) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: RFFT VJP now preserves cotangent dtype: (1) scale literal goes through new scale_by_n_matching_cotangent_dtype helper (mirror of the IRFFT one from 4y5q); (2) take_real_part generalized into take_real_part_as(value, target_dtype) with the existing entrypoint becoming a F64-fixed back-compat alias; (3) RFFT VJP derives real_dtype from the upstream input (F32 for F32/BF16/F16, F64 otherwise). New rfft_vjp_numerical_f32 test pins F32 dtype preservation + finite-diff correctness. fj-ad 272 + ad_numerical_verification 61 (incl. F64 RFFT + 3 IRFFT variants); clippy clean. Committed 4b76cb1.

title: fj-lax normalize_dynamic_start wraps to huge usize when window > dim
description: crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs:1814-1817 computes 'adjusted.max(0).min(dim - window) as usize'. When window > dim the expression 'dim - window' is negative i64, .min(negative) clamps the result to a negative i64, and the 'as usize' cast wraps to a huge value. Current callers (eval_dynamic_slice, eval_dynamic_update_slice) validate window <= dim BEFORE calling this helper, so the bug is latent rather than exploitable. Defensive harden so future callers cannot silently invoke the wrap-around. Acceptance: (a) compute max_offset as dim.saturating_sub(window).max(0) first so a negative result clamps to 0 before the i64-to-usize cast; (b) keep the existing semantics for window <= dim cases; (c) add a unit test that calls normalize_dynamic_start directly with window > dim and asserts the result is 0 (not a wrapped giant value); (d) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Defensive clamp added: max_start = (dim - window).max(0) before .min().as usize chain. Behavior unchanged for window <= dim. 3 unit tests cover window > dim clamp, existing-behavior preservation, and zero-window boundary. fj-lax 761 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed ede0eb9.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph max/min commutativity loses signed-zero order
description:
Review evidence:
- safe_algebraic_rules still includes max-comm/min-comm and max-assoc/min-assoc.
- fj-lax implements JAX/NumPy-style NaN propagation with f64::max/min after the NaN check (crates/fj-lax/src/lib.rs:137-151). Rust and NumPy preserve operand-order-visible signed zeros: max(-0.0, +0.0) returns +0.0, while max(+0.0, -0.0) returns -0.0; min has the same order-visible behavior.
- Treating max/min as commutative/associative in the safe egraph marks these distinct bit patterns equivalent, so extraction can change the sign bit even though safe mode is supposed to preserve observable boundaries.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add an egraph preservation test for out = Max(v1, v2) and out = Min(v1, v2).
2. Evaluate original and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr) with args [Value::scalar_f64(0.0), Value::scalar_f64(-0.0)].
3. Compare f64::to_bits(), not ==, because +0.0 == -0.0. Expected: optimized result preserves the original sign bit.
4. Add a 3-input associativity case: max(max(a,b),c) with signed-zero permutations and compare output bits.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_maxmin_zero RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics max_min_signed_zero_order -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: move max/min commutativity and associativity out of safe mode unless a canonical signed-zero policy is introduced and covered by conformance tests.
close_reason: Moved max/min commutativity and associativity out of safe mode; added signed-zero bit preservation regression.

title: fj-ad IRFFT VJP widens Complex64 input to Complex128 internally
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:2627-2655 IRFFT VJP computes 'inv_n = Literal::from_f64(1.0/fft_length as f64)' and multiplies it against the FFT of the cotangent. For a Complex64 input + F32 cotangent, FFT(g) is Complex64 but inv_n is F64; the value_mul promotes the result to Complex128, so the final gradient is Complex128 even though the input was Complex64. This wastes memory/precision in IRFFT VJP chains (downstream consumers may further promote everything), violates the dtype-preservation principle codified by frankenjax-kpqu, and silently changes type information across the AD boundary. Acceptance: (a) compute inv_n with a dtype matching the cotangent g: F32 cotangent → F32 inv_n (Literal::from_f32(1.0/n as f32)), F64 → F64 inv_n; (b) factor a helper 'fn reciprocal_scalar_matching_dtype(dtype: DType, n: usize) -> Value' for clarity and reuse; (c) update the test irfft_vjp_numerical_complex64 in fj-conformance/tests/ad_numerical_verification.rs to assert Complex64 dtype preservation (the current test was relaxed to Complex128 because of this bug); (d) confirm existing Complex128 IRFFT VJP test still passes; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean.
close_reason: Fix already landed at commit 2206c6f by peer cc-frankenjax with the exact same approach: new reciprocal_scalar_matching_cotangent_dtype helper emits F32 inv_n for F32/BF16/F16 cotangents and F64 otherwise, called from IRFFT VJP step 2. Conformance test irfft_vjp_numerical_complex64 now asserts Complex64 dtype preservation and reads VJP elements as Complex64Bits. My local commit was subsumed by the upstream peer commit during autostash-rebase (identical patch). fj-ad 272 tests + fj-conformance ad_numerical_verification 60 pass; clippy clean.

title: Implement complex rem and fix catch-all error messages in apply_complex_binary and eval_binary_elementwise_complex
description:
CONTEXT: apply_complex_binary() at fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:163-165 has a catch-all returning complex arithmetic not yet implemented for binary ops not yet handled. Max/min/pow are already implemented. The remaining ops hitting this catch-all are: Rem, Atan2, and any other binary op applied to complex (Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge, Dot, bitwise ops).

Similarly, eval_binary_elementwise_complex() at line ~255 has a gated allowlist. It currently allows Add/Sub/Mul/Div/Pow/Max/Min. Rem needs to be added to the allowlist.

SCOPE:
  - Rem (complex remainder): z1 % z2 = z1 - z2*round(z1/z2). Implement in apply_complex_binary.
  - Atan2: Not defined for complex. Change error to atan2 is not defined for complex operands.
  - Other binary ops (Eq, Ne, Lt, Le, Gt, Ge): These need complex-specific comparison. JAX compares by (real, imag) lexicographically for Eq/Ne, and raises errors for Lt/Le/Gt/Ge on complex. Change errors to not supported for complex (permanent, correct).
  - Dot: operates on tensors, handled elsewhere. Not in scope.
  - Bitwise ops: not applicable to complex. Change error to not supported for complex.
  - eval_binary_elementwise_complex: add Rem to the allowlist.

CHANGES: 1) Add Rem arm. 2) Change catch-all error from not yet implemented to unsupported for complex operands. 3) Add Rem to eval_binary_elementwise_complex allowlist.

TESTING: Test rem((4+3i), (2+1i)), test atan2(complex,complex) returns not defined error, test Lt(complex,complex) returns not supported error. Verify all existing tests pass.

ACCEPTANCE: Lines 163-165 and ~255 no longer contain not yet implemented. Catch-all says unsupported for complex operands.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph mul-neg-one changes promoted unsigned dtype
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `mul-neg-one` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at line around 359.
- The rewrite `mul(a, neg(1)) => neg(a)` is not dtype-preserving for promoted unsigned/integer inputs. The original `Mul` sees both operands and applies binary promotion; the rewritten program applies unary `Neg` to `a` first.
- `fj-lax` `eval_neg` supports unsigned inputs via wrapping negation, so the optimized program can return an unsigned wrapping value where the original promoted multiply returns a signed result.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Build a Jaxpr with `v2 = Neg(Literal::I64(1))` and `out = Mul(VarId(1), VarId(2))`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_u32(7)]`. Expected under current binary promotion: the multiply observes the I64 `-1` operand and returns the promoted signed result (not a U32 wrapping negation).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `Neg(VarId(1))`; `eval_neg` on U32 returns the wrapping U32 value.
4. Assert both value and dtype equality between original and safe/default optimized evaluations.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_mul_neg_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph mul_neg_one_dtype -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `mul-neg-one` out of safe mode until typed rewrites can prove the unary negation preserves the binary promoted output contract.
close_reason: Moved mul-neg-one out of safe mode and added promoted dtype regression coverage.

title: [code-review] safe egraph bitwise idempotence skips integer validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `bitwise-not-not`, `bitwise-and-self`, and `bitwise-or-self` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 448-450.
- `fj-lax` `eval_bitwise_binary` and unary bitwise evaluation reject non-integer scalar/tensor inputs and mismatched tensor dtype/shape.
- Safe egraph idempotence rewrites can remove those validation operations and return the original value directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression for `out = BitwiseAnd(x, x)` with one input `x`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_f64(1.0)]`. Expected: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing `TypeMismatch` (`bitwise ops require integer types`).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return `Value::scalar_f64(1.0)`.
4. Add a unary case `BitwiseNot(BitwiseNot(x))` with the same F64 input; original should error before the second not, while safe/default can rewrite to `x`.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_bitwise_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph bitwise_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move bitwise self/not-not rewrites out of safe mode or guard them behind typed integer evidence from an earlier validation/lowering phase.
close_reason: Moved bitwise validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: fj-cache persistence::serialize silently truncates payloads larger than u32::MAX
description: crates/fj-cache/src/persistence.rs:72 computes 'let payload_len = artifact.data.len() as u32'. For artifacts whose data exceeds u32::MAX (4 GiB-1), the cast silently truncates the declared length, the serialized bytes header undercounts the real payload, and subsequent deserialize() calls fail with LengthMismatch — the artifact is silently corrupted at write time. No realistic FrankenJAX workload caches 4GB+ artifacts today, but the silent truncation is defensively wrong. Acceptance: (a) add 'pub const MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE: usize = u32::MAX as usize' to the persistence module and re-export it; (b) document the constant in the wire-format header doc comment; (c) add a runtime check at the top of serialize() that asserts artifact.data.len() <= MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE via debug_assert! (debug-mode trip-wire) AND clamp via .min(MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE) before the u32 cast so release builds at least keep the header and body lengths agreeing (deserialize round-trip then reports cleanly via LengthMismatch instead of silently wrong-counting); (d) add a unit test that builds an artifact whose data.len() is MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE+1 (using a Vec-of-zeroes with usize > u32::MAX — only runnable on 64-bit targets) and asserts that the resulting bytes are still self-consistent (declared length == actual body length, even if both are truncated). The test should be #[cfg(target_pointer_width = "64")] gated and #[ignore]'d behind a feature flag to avoid 4GiB+ allocations in normal CI. (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added MAX_PAYLOAD_SIZE constant (u32::MAX as usize) with module-level doc. serialize() now uses debug_assert! trip-wire on oversize inputs and clamps effective_len in release builds so header+body+digest stay self-consistent; the SHA-256 digest is computed over the clamped slice. 2 new tests pin the constant and verify the header-length invariant. fj-cache 87 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 06f7403.

title: fj-ad scale_hermitian_adjoint zeros Complex64 interior bins in IRFFT VJP
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:702-737 scale_hermitian_adjoint doubles interior bins of an IRFFT VJP intermediate. The match handles Complex128Bits explicitly but falls through to a generic 'other => other.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0) * 2.0' branch for all other literal types. Literal::as_f64 returns None for Complex64Bits (see fj-core/src/lib.rs:557), so 'unwrap_or(0.0) * 2.0' produces 0 and the result is stored as Literal::from_f64 (F64Bits), zeroing out every interior bin AND changing the literal dtype away from Complex64. This silently corrupts the IRFFT VJP for any Complex64 (f32-complex) input. Acceptance: (a) add an explicit Complex64Bits arm that does 'let r = f32::from_bits(*re) * 2.0; let i = f32::from_bits(*im) * 2.0; elements[idx] = Literal::from_complex64(r, i);'; (b) the catchall 'other =>' arm currently mishandles real-valued types too — replace it with an Err(AdError::EvalFailed) since scale_hermitian_adjoint is only ever called on the IRFFT VJP path where elements MUST be complex; (c) add a regression unit test that constructs a Complex64 tensor with a known interior bin value (e.g., 1.0+2.0i at index 1 of length-4 input), calls scale_hermitian_adjoint with fft_length=6, and asserts the bin doubles to 2.0+4.0i and the dtype stays Complex64; (d) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added explicit Complex64Bits arm to scale_hermitian_adjoint that doubles bins via f32 math and preserves Complex64 dtype. Converted catchall 'other =>' from silent zero-out to AdError::EvalFailed (caller-bug surface). 2 regression tests: Complex64 fft_length=6 doubles interior bins correctly + F32 input now errors. fj-ad 270 tests + ad_numerical_verification 58 tests pass; clippy clean. title: [parity-gap] safe egraph identity rewrites still erase promoted dtypes
description:

4. Repeat for Mul(v1, Literal::I64(1)) and Pow(v1, Literal::I64(1)) over U32/U64.

title: Concatenate overflows output axis for zero-element huge dims
description:

Current debug/test builds panic when summing u32::MAX + 1; optimized builds can wrap the output axis to 0 and return the wrong shape.

Expected: use checked addition for the concatenated axis and return a typed EvalError when the output axis exceeds u32::MAX; also use checked multiplication for total output element count before allocating. Add a regression test using the zero-element huge-dim repro so it does not allocate.

title: [code-review] Egraph bitwise_xor self rewrite erases unsigned dtype
description:
Review finding from commit 1ccef8f in `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs`.

Defect: safe egraph rule `bitwise-xor-self` rewrites `(bitwise_xor ?a ?a)` to the bare egraph literal `0`. `egraph_to_jaxpr()` lowers bare numeric literals as `Literal::I64`, so optimizing an unsigned bitwise XOR self-cancel changes the output dtype/value representation from `U32(0)`/`U64(0)` to `I64(0)`. The type-blind egraph cannot safely introduce an untyped integer literal for typed bitwise operations.

Concrete repro:
1. Build a Jaxpr with one equation: `out = BitwiseXor(v1, v1)`.
2. Evaluate the original with `Value::scalar_u64(9_007_199_254_740_993)`: expected/output is `Value::scalar_u64(0)`.
3. Optimize with `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` and evaluate with the same input.
4. Actual optimized program returns an I64 zero (or otherwise violates unsigned dtype preservation) because the rewrite emits bare `Num(0)`.
5. Add/run a conformance regression with `rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics bitwise_xor_self_preserves_unsigned_dtype -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: remove `bitwise-xor-self` from the type-blind egraph rules, or add typed-zero lowering/proofs so U32/U64 inputs produce same-dtype zero. Cover scalar and tensor U32/U64 cases.
close_reason: Duplicate/subset of frankenjax-hf2l, which tracks the broader egraph literal dtype erasure issue including bitwise_xor unsigned zeros.

title: BroadcastInDim silently wraps oversized shape dims
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs eval_broadcast_in_dim parses the required shape param as i64, accepts any non-negative value, then casts with d as u32 at line 612. A shape dimension above u32::MAX silently wraps instead of returning an error. Repro: call eval_primitive(Primitive::BroadcastInDim, &[Value::scalar_i64(1)], &params) with params shape=4294967296. The implementation converts that dim to 0, computes total=0, and can return an empty tensor with shape [0] instead of rejecting the impossible requested output shape. Larger multi-dimensional shapes can also overflow the product at line 625/702 or attempt enormous allocation. Expected behavior: reject negative and out-of-u32-range dimensions before casting, and use checked multiplication for output element counts. Add regression tests covering shape=4294967296 and a product overflow case.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-range BroadcastInDim shape dims and checked output element products

The `cancels_shape_chain` function uses order-sensitive comparison for Rev axes:

```rust
(Primitive::Rev, Primitive::Rev) => rev_axes(&previous.params) == rev_axes(&current.params),
```

When axes are specified in different orders (e.g., `axes="0,1"` vs `axes="1,0"`), the comparison fails even though the operations are identical and should cancel.

**Fix:** Sort the axes before comparison or use a BTreeSet.
close_reason: Fixed Rev cancellation by canonicalizing axes and adding regression coverage

title: Pad overflows output dimension for huge zero-element shapes
description: eval_pad computes interior_span = (dim - 1) * interior and out_dim = low + dim + interior_span + high with unchecked i64 arithmetic. Expected: compute padded dimensions with checked arithmetic and return a typed EvalError on overflow; add a regression that does not allocate.

title: [code-review] safe egraph neg-neg skips bool validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `neg-neg` in `safe_algebraic_rules` while default optimization is safety mode.
- `fj-lax` `eval_neg` rejects bool scalar/tensor inputs.
- The safe rewrite `neg(neg(x)) => x` can remove both validation operations and return an invalid input directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Build a Jaxpr with one input `x` and equations `v2 = Neg(x)`, `out = Neg(v2)`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::Scalar(Literal::Bool(true))]`. Expected/current original behavior: `eval_jaxpr` returns `EvalError::TypeMismatch` from `eval_neg` with detail `expected numeric scalar, got bool`.
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return `Value::Scalar(Literal::Bool(true))`.
4. Add regression coverage beside the existing `numerical_safety_mode_preserves_*` tests and run `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_neg_neg_validation RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph neg_neg_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `neg-neg` out of safe mode, or require typed lowering evidence proving the inner neg validation already succeeded before eliminating both operations. Audit similar remaining safe unary rewrites such as `abs-neg`, trig/hyperbolic neg rules, `reciprocal-as-div`, and `pow-neg-one` for validation/dtype parity before declaring the family safe.
close_reason: Moved neg-neg to numerically_unsafe_rules; bypasses eval_neg bool validation

title: Handle complex max/min with magnitude comparison (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex max and min to eval_binary_elementwise_complex() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:179-191.
WHERE: The match at line 183-191 only allows add/sub/mul/div through; max/min hit the catch-all error at line 186.
HOW: JAX uses lexicographic comparison on (real, imag) for complex max/min. Implement: compare real parts first, then imag parts for tiebreaking. Return the element that "wins." This matches numpy.maximum/minimum behavior for complex.
WHY: Without this, vmap or reduce operations on complex tensors that use max/min will fail. JAX handles this case.
DEPENDS ON: None (independent of pow/rem/atan2).
TESTING: Test complex max/min: max((1+2i), (3+0i)) = (3+0i); min((1+2i), (1+1i)) = (1+1i). Edge case: equal real parts resolved by imag.
ACCEPTANCE: No "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" string in eval_binary_elementwise_complex for max/min.

title: Make BufferView slicing reject overflowing ranges
description:
Skill: /multi-pass-bug-hunting

Verified issue: crates/fj-runtime/src/buffer.rs BufferView::slice currently computes offset + len before checking bounds. For inputs such as offset = usize::MAX and len = 1, debug builds panic on arithmetic overflow and optimized builds can wrap before reaching the slice range, violating the documented contract that out-of-bounds ranges return None.

Reproduction: call BufferView::from_buffer(&Buffer::new(vec![1, 2, 3], DeviceId(0))).slice(usize::MAX, 1). The current implementation panics instead of returning None.

Expected fix: use checked_add for the end offset, return None on overflow, and keep existing valid/boundary slice behavior unchanged. Add a unit test covering the overflow case.
close_reason: Already fixed in commit 9ad7ba8: BufferView::slice now uses checked_add and returns None on overflow

title: fj-core Jaxpr::validate_well_formed does not recurse into equation sub_jaxprs
description: crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:1271 Jaxpr::validate_well_formed iterates only self.equations top-level — it never recurses into equation.sub_jaxprs (the bodies attached to control-flow primitives like Cond, Scan, While). Malformed sub_jaxprs (duplicate bindings, unbound input vars, outvars not declared) are not caught at validation time and surface much later as interpreter/eval errors, or worse, silently corrupt control-flow execution. Acceptance: (a) extend validate_well_formed to call self.validate_well_formed() recursively on every equation.sub_jaxprs entry; (b) propagate any nested JaxprValidationError up to the caller (a nested error reuses the top-level error type — no new variants needed); (c) add a unit test that builds a parent Jaxpr whose Cond equation has a sub_jaxpr with DuplicateBinding on its invars, and asserts validate_well_formed returns the nested error; (d) also assert that well-formed parents containing well-formed sub_jaxprs still pass; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch. Note: validate_well_formed's existing return type already supports recursion (Result<(), JaxprValidationError>) so this is a non-breaking semantics tightening.
close_reason: Extended Jaxpr::validate_well_formed to recurse into equation.sub_jaxprs via sub_jaxpr.validate_well_formed()?. Sub-jaxpr scopes remain independent (no parent bindings propagated). 2 new tests: nested DuplicateBinding propagates + well-formed body passes. Full workspace tests green; fj-core 116 tests; clippy clean. Committed 4715e38.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph reciprocal-reciprocal erases subnormal overflow
description:
Review evidence:
- Commits 2971d17, 348837a, 2ab703f, and 1ccef8f moved several unsafe egraph identities behind OptimizationConfig::aggressive().
- safe_algebraic_rules still contains reciprocal-reciprocal: (reciprocal (reciprocal ?a)) => ?a.
- That identity is not safe for tiny nonzero floats: 1.0 / 1e-320 overflows to +Inf, then 1.0 / +Inf is +0.0, while the rewrite returns 1e-320.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph unit test building chained_unary_jaxpr(Primitive::Reciprocal, Primitive::Reciprocal).

title: Implement complex pow in apply_complex_binary (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)
description:
WHAT: Add complex exponentiation to apply_complex_binary() in crates/fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:145-164.
WHERE: The match arm at line 160 currently returns "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" for all binary ops except add/sub/mul/div.
HOW: Complex pow uses the identity (a+bi)^(c+di) = exp((c+di)*ln(a+bi)). Decompose: r=|z|, theta=atan2(b,a), ln(z)=(ln(r), theta), then exp of the product. For integer exponents, use repeated squaring for efficiency.
WHY: JAX supports complex pow natively. Without it, grad(f) fails for any traced program that involves complex exponentiation.
TESTING: Add tests in fj-lax for complex pow at several points: (1+i)^2 = 2i, (1+i)^(0.5), purely real complex pow matches real pow. Add numerical AD verification for complex pow VJP.
ACCEPTANCE: The "complex arithmetic not yet implemented" error no longer triggers for Primitive::Pow with complex inputs.

title: Conv panics on zero stride parameter
description:
[HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs accepts strides=0 for Primitive::Conv and then performs division by that stride instead of returning a typed EvalError. In 1D conv, eval_conv_1d parses params["strides"] at lines 2817-2820 and then uses width.div_ceil(stride) for SAME padding at line 2824 or (width - kernel_w) / stride for VALID padding at line 2835. In 2D conv, parse_stride_pair returns zero at lines 2976-2986 and compute_output_and_pad divides by stride at lines 2997 and 3005.

Repro: construct lhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 3, 1] }, three F32 literals) and rhs TensorValue::new(DType::F32, Shape { dims: vec![1, 1, 1] }, one F32 literal), set params { "strides": "0", "padding": "valid" }, then call eval_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs, rhs], &params). Current debug/test builds panic on division by zero; release builds can also compute invalid output geometry.

Expected: reject zero or malformed strides before arithmetic with a typed EvalError::Unsupported such as "conv stride must be positive". Add regression tests for 1D VALID, 1D SAME, and 2D zero-stride inputs.
close_reason: Rejected zero and malformed Conv strides before output geometry arithmetic

title: Fuse partially-overlapping Rev∘Rev pairs in egraph shape-chain prepass
description: optimize_shape_parametric_chains currently cancels Rev∘Rev only when both equations specify the exact same axis set (after sort/dedup via rev_axes). Two consecutive Rev equations on a single-use intermediate with axis sets A and B that overlap partially are equivalent to one Rev with axis set (A ⊕ B) — symmetric difference — because each axis in A ∩ B is reversed twice (no-op) while each axis in A ⊕ B is reversed exactly once. Acceptance: (a) extend fuses_shape_chain with (Rev, Rev) gated on a non-empty symmetric difference and add a sym-diff path in the fuse branch that rewrites current's axes param to the deduplicated sorted symmetric difference; (b) the existing cancellation branch already handles A == B, so the new fuse path only fires for partial overlap; (c) add unit tests for partial overlap (rank-3 [0,1] then [1,2] -> [0,2]) and for the empty-overlap case ([0] then [1] -> [0,1]); (d) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Implemented Rev∘Rev partial-overlap fusion: new rev_axes_symmetric_difference returns Some(sym-diff) iff both sides parse; extended fuses_shape_chain with (Rev, Rev) gated on non-empty sym-diff (cancellation branch handles A==B case); fuse branch rewrites current's axes param to sym-diff CSV. Added 2 unit tests (rank-3 partial overlap [0,1]+[1,2]->[0,2] and disjoint [0]+[1]->[0,1]) with eval-equivalence assertions. Verified: fj-egraph 134 tests, conformance preserves_semantics 33 tests, clippy clean. Committed 036a25a.

title: [code-review] Safe egraph reciprocal involution changes subnormal behavior
description:
Review finding from commits 1ccef8f/2ab703f in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.

Defect: `safe_algebraic_rules()` still includes `reciprocal-reciprocal`; default `optimize_jaxpr()` now uses `OptimizationConfig::safe()`, so the safe/default optimizer rewrites `reciprocal(reciprocal(x))` to `x`. That is not safe at subnormal boundaries. For f64 `x = 1e-320`, the original program computes `1.0 / (1.0 / x)`: the inner reciprocal overflows to +Inf and the outer reciprocal returns +0.0. The optimized program returns 1e-320. This violates the config docs that safe mode protects denormals and violates JAX/IEEE observable behavior for the unfused operation.

Concrete repro:
1. Add a conformance test in `crates/fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs` that builds `v2 = reciprocal(v1); v3 = reciprocal(v2)`.
2. Evaluate the original Jaxpr and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with `Value::scalar_f64(1e-320)`.
3. Expected: safe/default optimization preserves the original +0.0 result, or keeps the reciprocal chain. Actual: optimized output is 1e-320 because the rule removes both reciprocals.
4. Run with `rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-conformance --test egraph_preserves_semantics reciprocal_subnormal -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move `reciprocal-reciprocal` into `numerically_unsafe_rules()` unless a range/type proof is available, and add the conformance test above.
close_reason: Duplicate of frankenjax-elh8, which tracks the same safe egraph reciprocal-reciprocal subnormal parity gap.

title: Fix large I64 precision loss in sort and argsort
description:
Review finding [HIGH]. In crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs, sort_along_axis currently converts integral tensor elements to f64 before sorting (lines around 2589 and 2611-2639). Values above 2^53 cannot be represented exactly as f64, so Sort can silently rewrite distinct I64 literals when it casts the sorted f64 back to i64, and Argsort can compare rounded values instead of the original integer keys.

Reproduction to add as a failing test: evaluate Primitive::Sort on an I64 tensor [9007199254740993, 9007199254740992]. Expected exact sorted values are [9007199254740992, 9007199254740993], but the current implementation routes both values through f64 and can collapse/corrupt the larger literal. Also evaluate Primitive::Argsort on [9007199254740993, 9007199254740992, 9007199254740994] and assert the indices reflect true i64 ordering, not f64-rounded ordering.

Suggested fix: sort integral tensors with an i64 key path that preserves the original Literal for Sort and uses the same exact key for Argsort. Keep the f64 total_cmp path for floating tensors so NaN ordering remains covered by existing tests. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-lax sort and rch cargo test -p fj-conformance --test sort_oracle.
close_reason: Fixed exact integer sort and argsort ordering with oracle regressions

title: Implement complex unary ops in eval_unary_elementwise (fj-lax arithmetic.rs)

CONTEXT: eval_unary_elementwise() at fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs has TWO stubs (scalar at ~line 1038, tensor at ~line 1075) returning complex arithmetic not yet implemented when a unary op receives Complex64/Complex128 input.

SCOPE: For ops that JAX supports on complex (sign, square, reciprocal, is_finite): implement the complex logic. For ops JAX does NOT support on complex (floor, ceil, round, lgamma, digamma, erf, erfc, erf_inv): change error to not supported for complex dtype (permanent correct error, not deferred).

CHANGES: 1) Scalar match (line ~1038): dispatch Complex64/Complex128 to per-op complex implementations. 2) Tensor match (line ~1075): same dispatch for tensor elements. 3) Ops unsupported on complex get descriptive permanent error.

TESTING: sign(1+1i)=(1+1i)/sqrt(2), reciprocal(2+0i)=0.5+0i, is_finite(1+2i)=true, floor(complex) returns not supported error. Verify all existing tests pass; log before/after test counts.

ACCEPTANCE: Lines ~1038 and ~1075 no longer contain not yet implemented.
close_reason: Completed: implemented supported complex unary ops and replaced remaining unary complex stubs with permanent descriptive errors; rch cargo test -p fj-lax passed

title: [parity-gap] egraph literal rewrites erase output dtype
description:
Review evidence:
- The egraph language stores bare numeric rewrite constants as FjLang::Num(i64), and id_to_atom lowers every FjLang::Num back to Literal::I64.
- safe_algebraic_rules has literal-producing rewrites such as is-finite-const-0/1 => 1, bitwise-xor-self => 0, pow-zero => 1, and integer-pow-zero => 1.
- Existing tests around these rules mostly assert equation count, not output dtype/value parity after eval.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a fj-egraph unit test for unary_jaxpr(Primitive::IsFinite, Atom::Lit(Literal::I64(0))).
2. Compare eval_jaxpr(original, &[]) to eval_jaxpr(optimize_jaxpr(&original), &[]).
3. Expected original output is Value::scalar_bool(true); current optimized literal path can produce Literal::I64(1).
4. Add the same check for bitwise_xor(x, x) with a U32/U64 input and pow(f64_x, Literal::I64(0)); both should preserve the reference output dtype.
5. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_egraph_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph egraph_literal_dtype -- --nocapture
close_reason: Moved dtype-erasing rewrites (pow-zero, integer-pow-zero, bitwise-xor-self, is-finite-const-0/1) to numerically_unsafe_rules

title: [code-review] egraph lowering panics on malformed Jaxpr inputs
description: Recent egraph safety commits were reviewed for panic surfaces. jaxpr_to_egraph is public and still indexes malformed graphs directly: Atom::Var(var) lowers via var_map[var] in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs, and each supported primitive then indexes input_ids[0], input_ids[1], or input_ids[2] without checking equation arity. optimize_supported_segment catches EGraphLoweringError but cannot catch these panics. Add explicit missing-variable and arity validation plus adversarial malformed-Jaxpr tests.

title: Reshape silently wraps oversized new_shape dims
description:
[HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs eval_reshape parses the new_shape parameter as i64 but casts non-negative dimensions with `as u32` before checking range. This is still present at lines 401-414 for scalar inputs and lines 445-479 for tensor inputs. A requested dimension above u32::MAX silently wraps instead of returning EvalError, which is the same bug class recently fixed for BroadcastInDim/Concatenate/Pad output shapes.

Repro: construct an empty tensor with `TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![])`, wrap it as `Value::Tensor`, set params `{ "new_shape": "4294967296" }`, and call `eval_primitive(Primitive::Reshape, &[input], &params)`. Current code casts 4294967296 to 0, computes total 0, and can return shape `[0]` instead of rejecting the impossible requested shape. Scalar reshape has the same unchecked cast path and can also mis-handle out-of-range dimensions before validating product.

Expected: reject negative and out-of-u32-range dims before casting, use checked multiplication for known/output products, reject inferred dimensions that exceed u32::MAX, and add regression tests for `new_shape=4294967296` plus product overflow cases that do not allocate.
close_reason: Rejected out-of-u32 reshape dims and checked reshape products before casting

title: Add Reshape∘Reshape fusion to fj-egraph shape-chain prepass
description: In crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs::optimize_shape_parametric_chains, two consecutive Reshape equations on the same value (single-output, single-use intermediate) can be fused into one Reshape with the second's new_shape param. The intermediate Reshape's only effect on the carried value is to update shape metadata; if the next equation also Reshapes the same data, the intermediate shape is unobservable and the fused single Reshape produces the same final shape and elements. Currently cancels_shape_chain only handles Transpose∘Transpose, ExpandDims∘Squeeze, Squeeze∘ExpandDims, and Rev∘Rev — Reshape chains are NOT optimized. Acceptance: (a) extend the prepass with a fuse_shape_chain branch for (Reshape, Reshape) that drops the previous Reshape and keeps current; preserve aliasing semantics like cancellation; (b) the fusion only triggers when previous Reshape's output is single-use; (c) add a unit test in fj-egraph's existing test module verifying the fusion fires; (d) add a metamorphic test in egraph_preserves_semantics.rs showing chained Reshape on a 6-element tensor reshaped 6 → 2x3 → 3x2 produces the same TensorValue as the unoptimized jaxpr; (e) workspace clippy + tests must remain green via rch.
close_reason: Implemented Reshape∘Reshape fusion in optimize_shape_parametric_chains: new fuses_shape_chain helper + fuse branch in the prepass loop that drops single-use intermediate Reshapes and rewires inputs. Added 2 unit tests in fj-egraph (positive fusion + extra-consumer guard) and 2 metamorphic tests in fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs (concrete fusion + inferred-dim path). All fj-egraph (129), fj-conformance::egraph_preserves_semantics (33), fj-dispatch+fj-api (398) tests pass via rch; clippy clean. Committed 2df8fe5.

title: fj-ad scan_vjp force-converts all xs gradients to F64 and silently zeros complex
description: crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs:3707-3736 scan_vjp initializes g_xs_elements with Literal::from_f64(0.0) and then writes each step's xs gradient via 'Literal::from_f64(lit.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0))' before wrapping in TensorValue::new(DType::F64, ...). Two problems: (1) For real-valued inputs (f32, i64, u32, bf16, f16) this works but loses dtype precision — the scan VJP always returns f64 regardless of xs dtype, breaking downstream type-matching expectations. (2) For Complex64/Complex128 inputs, Literal::as_f64 returns None, so 'unwrap_or(0.0)' silently zeros every element of the xs gradient and the final tensor is misreported as DType::F64 even though the input was complex. Same pattern in the scalar arm (line 3725). Acceptance: (a) preserve the input dtype: declare 'let out_dtype = xs_tensor.dtype' once, use it in the final TensorValue::new; (b) replace the per-step Literal::from_f64(lit.as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0)) writes with direct copies of step_grads[1] elements (cloning the Literal); (c) initialize g_xs_elements with a dtype-appropriate zero literal helper (a small match on xs_tensor.dtype emitting Literal::I64(0), Literal::from_f64(0.0), Literal::Complex64Bits(0, 0), etc.); (d) add a regression unit test that scans add over a Complex64 xs and asserts the resulting xs gradient (a) has dtype Complex64, (b) is not all-zero (use a non-trivial gradient input); (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: scan_vjp now copies step VJP gradients verbatim instead of coercing via as_f64().unwrap_or(0.0). New observed_grad_dtype tracks the body's actual output dtype, propagated as the output TensorValue dtype; mixed-dtype scan bodies error out (defensive). dtype_for_literal helper added. 2 regression tests: f32 preserves dtype/element alignment + Complex64 grad with complex cotangent stays Complex64 AND not all-zero. fj-ad 272 tests + ad_numerical_verification 58 + dispatch_ad_oracle 19 pass; clippy clean. Committed 86497e4.

title: Optimize DFT twiddle factor computation - precompute roots of unity
description:
**Performance issue in fj-lax/src/fft.rs:**

The O(n²) DFT fallback computes sin_cos() for every (j,k) pair:
```rust
for (k, out) in output.iter_mut().enumerate() {
    for (j, &(xr, xi)) in input.iter().enumerate() {
        let angle = -2.0 * PI * (j as f64) * (k as f64) / (n as f64);
        let (sin_a, cos_a) = angle.sin_cos();  // Called n² times!
```

**Fix:** Precompute the n-th roots of unity once (n calls), then lookup by `(j*k) % n`.
This reduces trig calls from O(n²) to O(n) for non-power-of-2 lengths.

**Skill applied:** /profiling-software-performance
close_reason: Precomputed n-th roots of unity in DFT, reducing trig calls from O(n²) to O(n). Commit d0114ff.

title: Split panics on zero num_sections for empty axes
description:
`eval_split` accepts `num_sections=0` because `parse_usize_param` allows zero and the Split path never validates that the section count is positive. For an empty split axis, current Rust reports `0usize.is_multiple_of(0) == true`, so `eval_split` reaches `let section_size = axis_size / num_sections` and divides by zero at `crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs:3550`.

Reproduction sketch:
1. Build an empty tensor, for example `TensorValue::new(DType::I64, Shape { dims: vec![0] }, vec![])` wrapped as `Value::Tensor`.
2. Call `eval_primitive(Primitive::Split, &[input], &BTreeMap::from([("num_sections".into(), "0".into())]))`.
3. Expected: `Err(EvalError::Unsupported { primitive: Primitive::Split, detail: ... })` because zero sections is invalid.
4. Actual: panic from integer division by zero in `eval_split`.

The recent Split oracle suite in `crates/fj-conformance/tests/split_oracle.rs` covers positive section counts but does not include invalid zero-section coverage. Fix should reject `num_sections == 0` before divisibility/reshape logic and add regression coverage for empty-axis and non-empty-axis inputs.
close_reason: Fixed: added explicit check rejecting num_sections==0 before divisibility logic in eval_split. Added 3 regression tests for zero sections on both empty and non-empty axes.

title: [code-review] safe egraph clamp rewrites bypass validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `clamp-to-minmax` and `clamp-same` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 431-433.
- `fj-lax` `eval_clamp` rejects complex/bool literals and rejects unsupported scalar/tensor input forms before computing values.
- Safe egraph rewrites can bypass those checks: `clamp(a, a, a) => a`, or `clamp(x, lo, hi) => min(max(x, lo), hi)` can route through broader binary broadcasting semantics instead of clamp's stricter contract.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression that builds `out = Clamp(x, x, x)` with one input `x`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::Scalar(Literal::from_complex128(1.0, 0.0))]`. Expected: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing clamp `TypeMismatch` (`clamp is not supported for complex dtypes`).
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to `x` and return the complex value.
4. Add a `clamp-to-minmax` shape/kind case such as scalar `x` with tensor `lo`/`hi`; `eval_clamp` rejects unsupported input forms, while transformed max/min may broadcast and succeed.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_clamp_safe_rules RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph clamp_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move clamp rewrites out of safe mode until egraph carries enough dtype, kind, and shape evidence to prove the original `eval_clamp` validation has already succeeded.
close_reason: Moved clamp validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: Make batched fixture timeouts return without joining timed-out workers
description:
Review finding [HIGH]. Commit 0985d27 changed run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched in crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs to record timed-out worker handles, but the function then joins all timed_out_handles before returning (current lines around 1145-1186). That means case_timeout does not bound wall-clock runtime: a timed-out case with a long or hung worker still blocks the whole batched run at the final join.

Reproduction to add: construct a TransformFixtureBundle with one case having simulated_delay_ms = 5000 and BatchRunnerConfig { case_timeout: Duration::from_millis(1) }. Measure elapsed time for run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched. Expected behavior for a timeout runner is to return a Timeout report promptly after roughly the configured timeout; current behavior waits for the delayed worker during the final join and returns only after the worker finishes. A genuinely hung worker would hang the harness indefinitely despite emitting a timeout report.

Suggested fix: preserve bounded fan-out without joining timed-out workers on the critical return path. Options include a scoped worker pool with cooperative cancellation, a persistent bounded runner, or clearly documenting and renaming the timeout as classification-only. Add an elapsed-time regression test so future code cannot reintroduce timeout-as-blocking-join semantics. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance test_batch_runner.
close_reason: Detached timed-out batch workers from the return path and added elapsed-time regression coverage

title: Avoid holding LRU order lock during backend eviction
description:
Skill: /deadlock-finder-and-fixer

Verified hazard: crates/fj-cache/src/eviction.rs LruCache::enforce_budget holds the internal order Mutex while invoking CacheBackend::stats and CacheBackend::evict. CacheBackend is a public trait for arbitrary backends, so calling backend code while holding the wrapper lock creates a reentrancy/deadlock hazard if a backend observes or re-enters the LRU wrapper, logs through shared cache state, or blocks behind code that needs the order lock.

Reproduction target: add a custom test backend whose stats/evict methods try_lock the LruCache order mutex. On the current implementation, inserting beyond max_entries or max_bytes fails because enforce_budget still holds order while calling into the backend.

Expected fix: shorten the order critical section, pop eviction keys into local values, drop the lock, and only then call backend stats/evict. Keep LRU ordering behavior unchanged and add a regression test that proves backend calls are made without the order lock held.
close_reason: Completed: moved LRU budget eviction backend calls outside the order mutex critical section and added a reentrancy regression test proving backend stats/evict run without the order lock held.

title: [parity-gap] safe egraph pow-two rewrites erase promoted dtypes
description:
Review evidence from the last-six-hours egraph safety commits:
- 14fcdc9 moved add/sub zero, mul/div/pow/integer_pow one, and max/min commutativity/associativity out of safe mode, but safe_algebraic_rules still keeps pow-two and integer-pow-two in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs.
- Primitive::Pow evaluates through fj-lax binary dtype promotion. For a U32 input and an I64 literal exponent 2, promote_dtype(U32, I64) => I64, so the original Jaxpr returns Value::Scalar(Literal::I64(49)).
- The safe egraph rule pow-two rewrites pow(x, 2) to mul(x, x). Evaluating Mul over two U32 operands returns Value::Scalar(Literal::U32(49)), so safe/default optimization changes the observable dtype.
- Primitive::IntegerPow currently returns F64 for numeric scalar/tensor inputs. The safe egraph rule integer-pow-two rewrites integer_pow(x, exponent=2) to mul(x, x), so a U32 input changes from F64(49.0) to U32(49).

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add cases to numerical_safety_mode_preserves_promoted_dtype_identity_boundaries in crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs for Pow(VarId(1), Literal::I64(2)) and integer_pow_jaxpr(2) with args [Value::scalar_u32(7)].
2. Evaluate the original with eval_jaxpr; expected outputs are I64(49) for Pow and F64(49.0) for IntegerPow under the current fj-lax contracts.
3. Evaluate optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe()) and optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr); current safe/default can emit Mul(var,var) and return U32(49).
4. Run: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_pow_two_dtype RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph numerical_safety_mode_preserves_promoted_dtype_identity_boundaries -- --nocapture

Suggested fix: move pow-two and integer-pow-two to numerically_unsafe_rules until egraph lowering can prove and preserve the result dtype, or replace them with typed rewrites that keep the promoted output contract.
close_reason: Moved pow-two and integer-pow-two out of safe mode; added promoted dtype regression coverage.

title: Fuse non-inverse Transpose∘Transpose pairs in egraph shape-chain prepass
description: optimize_shape_parametric_chains currently cancels Transpose∘Transpose only when the pair is inverse (composed permutation is identity). Non-inverse Transpose∘Transpose pairs on a single-use intermediate could be fused into one Transpose carrying the composed permutation, halving the equation count and reducing downstream egraph saturation work. Composition: composed[j] = perm_b[perm_a[j]] where perm_b is the previous (innermost) transpose and perm_a is the current (outermost). Acceptance: (a) extend fuses_shape_chain with (Transpose, Transpose) and add a permutation-composition path in the fuse branch that rewrites current's permutation param and rewires current's input atom to previous's input atom; (b) only fuse when BOTH transposes carry an explicit permutation param (skip when either is the implicit reverse-axes form to keep the change conservative); (c) add unit tests for the (rank-3 non-inverse) fuse case and the (one-side-reverse) skip case in fj-egraph; (d) extend the existing reshape-chain proptest into a generic shape-chain proptest that includes Transpose mixed with Reshape (or add a sister proptest); (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.

title: Bound batched fixture timeout thread fan-out
description: The fj-conformance batched transform fixture runner in crates/fj-conformance/src/lib.rs currently clones the entire bundle, spawns one OS thread per case up front, and then waits on each receiver with a timeout. Large oracle bundles can therefore create hundreds of threads immediately, and timed-out cases keep running in detached background threads. Change run_transform_fixture_bundle_batched so it starts each case only when it is being waited on and joins successful case threads after receiving their result, preserving report order while preventing all cases from fanning out at once. Add regression coverage with multiple delayed cases to prove timeout reporting still preserves case order. Validate with rch cargo test -p fj-conformance test_batch_runner, rch cargo check -p fj-conformance --all-targets, rch cargo clippy -p fj-conformance --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo fmt --check, and ubs on the touched file.
close_reason: Changed the batched transform fixture runner to start each case only when waiting on it, join completed worker threads, retain timeout reporting order, and added delayed-plus-fast regression coverage. title: [code-review] safe egraph complex rewrites skip dtype validation
description:
Review evidence from the six-hour egraph hardening window:
- `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` still keeps `real-complex`, `imag-complex`, and `complex-real-imag` in `safe_algebraic_rules` at lines around 436-438.
- `fj-lax` `eval_real`/`eval_imag` reject non-complex inputs, and `eval_complex` validates part dtypes/shapes before constructing a complex value.
- Safe egraph extraction can remove those validation operations and return an input variable directly.

Concrete repro steps:
1. Add a regression in `crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs` or `crates/fj-conformance/tests/egraph_preserves_semantics.rs` that builds `out = Complex(Real(z), Imag(z))` with one input `z`.
2. Evaluate the original with `args = [Value::scalar_f64(3.0)]`. Expected under the current fj-lax contract: `eval_jaxpr` returns the existing `TypeMismatch` from `Real`/`Imag` because the input is not complex.
3. Evaluate `optimize_jaxpr_with_config(&jaxpr, &OptimizationConfig::safe())` and `optimize_jaxpr(&jaxpr)` with the same args. Current safe/default can rewrite to just `z` and return `Value::scalar_f64(3.0)`, converting an input validation error into success.
4. Add a second case for `Real(Complex(r, i))` with `r = Value::scalar_f64(1.0)` and `i = Value::scalar_bool(true)`. Original `Complex` should reject the bool part; safe/default can rewrite to `r`.
5. Run: `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/data/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_complex_safe_rules RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo test -p fj-egraph complex_validation -- --nocapture`.

Suggested fix: move the complex safe rewrites to `numerically_unsafe_rules` or a typed/validated lowering phase that proves complex input and part validity before eliminating operations.
close_reason: Moved complex validation-skipping egraph rewrites out of safe mode and added validation regression coverage.

title: fj-cache FileCache::evict and clear() silently swallow filesystem errors

close_reason: Extended silent-failure-counter pattern from put() to evict() and clear(). evict() now distinguishes NotFound (normal false return) from other IO errors via ErrorKind. clear() now records read_dir whole-dir failures and per-entry remove failures. title: fj-cache FileCache::put silently swallows rename failures
description: crates/fj-cache/src/backend.rs:150-164 implements atomic write via temp-file + rename. The rename step uses 'let _ = std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &path)' which silently discards rename errors (disk-full, permission, cross-device link, concurrent removal of parent dir). When rename fails after the temp write succeeded, the cache silently fails to store the artifact and the caller has no way to detect it. The trait signature 'fn put(&mut self, ...)' returns () so we cannot propagate the error directly without breaking callers. Acceptance: (a) add a 'put_failures: AtomicU64' counter field to FileCache; (b) increment the counter on either std::fs::write failure or std::fs::rename failure inside put(); (c) expose the count via a new method 'pub fn put_failure_count(&self) -> u64' on FileCache (non-trait, non-breaking); (d) add a unit test that triggers a put_failure by attempting to put under a path whose parent directory has been removed mid-flight, asserting the counter increments and downstream stats() entry_count stays unchanged; (e) workspace tests + clippy clean via rch.
close_reason: Added put_failures AtomicU64 + put_failure_count() accessor to FileCache. Replaced let-underscore-discard branches in put() with explicit match that increments counter on temp-write OR rename failure, with best-effort cleanup. Added 2 unit tests (NotFound parent dir bumps counter; healthy put keeps it 0). Verified: fj-cache 81 tests pass; clippy clean. Committed 9992982.

title: [modes-of-reasoning] numerical-stability-vs-JIT-correctness
description:
E-graph rewrites (exp-log, div-self, reassociation) are mathematically valid but numerically treacherous near domain boundaries. Eager vs JIT diverge on edge cases (0, denormal, NaN, Inf). Conformance detects drift post-hoc but cannot prevent unsafe rewrites.

Key locations:
- fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:269-324 (rewrite rules without domain guards)
- fj-lax/src/arithmetic.rs:219-222 (underflow-prone complex reciprocal)
- fj-conformance/src/numerical_stability.rs:857-920 (tolerance enforcement gap)

Suggested work:
1. Add domain predicates to e-graph rules (div-self only when provably non-zero)
2. Implement numerical safety mode that disables associativity rewrites
3. Extend conformance gate to compare JIT vs eager on edge-case inputs
close_reason: Added OptimizationConfig with numerical_safety_mode flag in ceea42f. Safe mode disables 6 unsafe rewrites: exp-log, div-self, mul-reciprocal, div-mul-cancel, log-product, log-quotient.

title: Conv SAME padding panics on empty spatial dimensions
description: [HIGH] crates/fj-lax/src/tensor_ops.rs Conv SAME padding assumes the computed output spatial dimension is nonzero. For empty spatial axes, width.div_ceil(stride) or input_size.div_ceil(stride) can return 0, then line 3123 and helper line 3324 compute out_w - 1 / out - 1 and underflow in debug/test builds. Repro 1D: lhs TensorValue::new(DType::F64, Shape { dims: vec![1,0,1] }, vec![]), rhs TensorValue::new(DType::F64, Shape { dims: vec![1,1,1] }, vec![1.0]), params { padding="same", strides="1" }, then eval_primitive(Primitive::Conv, &[lhs, rhs], &params). Expected: either an empty output tensor with shape [1,0,1] or a typed EvalError, but no panic. Actual: SAME geometry computes out_w=0 and underflows at ((out_w - 1) * stride + kernel_w). The 2D path uses compute_output_and_pad and has the same out == 0 underflow for any empty height/width with SAME padding. Add regression tests for 1D width=0 and 2D height/width=0.
close_reason: Fixed: added early return for input_size=0 in compute_output_and_pad and 1D conv path. Added 5 regression tests for empty spatial dims.

title: Replace e-graph not-yet-supported comment with principled UnsupportedPrimitive categorization
description:
CONTEXT: crates/fj-egraph/src/lib.rs:598 has comment Shape ops require params -- not yet supported followed by 34 primitives returning UnsupportedPrimitive. The not yet wording is misleading -- these are excluded BY DESIGN.

CHANGES REQUIRED:
  1. Replace comment with accurate doc: these primitives are architecturally excluded because the s-expression egraph cannot represent parameterized operations (shapes, axes, sub-jaxprs).
  2. Split the 34-primitive catch-all into categorized groups with per-group doc comments:
     - Shape manipulation (13): Reshape, Slice, DynamicSlice, Gather, Scatter, Transpose, BroadcastInDim, Concatenate, Pad, Rev, Squeeze, Split, ExpandDims
     - Linear algebra (5): Cholesky, Qr, Svd, TriangularSolve, Eigh
     - FFT (4): Fft, Ifft, Rfft, Irfft
     - Control flow (4): Cond, Scan, While, Switch
     - Sorting (2): Sort, Argsort
     - Convolution (1): Conv
     - Index/utility (3): Iota, BroadcastedIota, DynamicUpdateSlice
     - Type conversion (2): BitcastConvertType, ReducePrecision
     - Cumulative (2): Cumsum, Cumprod
     - Windowed (1): ReduceWindow
     - Encoding (1): OneHot
  3. Add ExclusionReason enum or string field to EGraphLoweringError::UnsupportedPrimitive so error messages say e.g. reshape excluded: requires shape parameters (not representable in algebraic e-graph).
  4. Add test: for each category, verify the error message contains the category reason.

TESTING: Existing egraph tests must still pass. New test: construct Jaxpr with each excluded primitive, call jaxpr_to_egraph, assert error message contains category keyword. Log all 34 exclusion messages.

ACCEPTANCE: The string not yet supported gone. Each exclusion has a documented reason. Error messages are informative.
close_reason: Centralized egraph exclusion metadata by category, surfaced reasons in UnsupportedPrimitive errors, added full exclusion-message coverage, and verified with rch cargo test -p fj-egraph plus workspace check/clippy/fmt

title: Repair fj-conformance fuzz coverage for pmap enum additions
description:
testing-fuzzing audit finding from cod_4.

Evidence:
- `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenjax_cod_4_fuzz RCH_ENV_ALLOWLIST=CARGO_TARGET_DIR rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml` fails with E0004 in `crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/fuzz_targets/common.rs:257`: missing `Primitive::Psum`, `Primitive::Pmean`, `Primitive::AllGather`, `Primitive::AllToAll`, and `Primitive::AxisIndex`.
- `fj-core::Primitive` defines those pmap collective variants at `crates/fj-core/src/lib.rs:196-201`; `common.rs` `ALL_PRIMITIVES` and `primitive_arity` only cover pre-pmap variants.
- `fj-core::Transform` includes `Transform::Pmap`, but `sample_transform` in `common.rs:241-246` samples only `Jit`, `Grad`, and `Vmap`, so dispatch/composition fuzzing never exercises the V1 pmap fail-closed path.

Acceptance criteria:
- Update the fuzz helper manually so primitive sampling and arity handling are exhaustive for the current `Primitive` enum, including pmap collectives.
- Include `Transform::Pmap` in transform fuzz sampling with expectations that pmap dispatch fails closed rather than panicking.
- Add/extend helper-level tests or compile checks so future enum additions break clearly.
- Verify with `rch exec -- cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml`.
close_reason: Fixed: fuzz helper now covers pmap collective primitives and samples Transform::Pmap; added fuzz_common test target. Verified cargo check --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml --all-targets and cargo test --manifest-path crates/fj-conformance/fuzz/Cargo.toml --test fuzz_common via rch. cargo test -p fj-conformance is blocked by unrelated stale README artifact hash in e2e_forensic_log_contract.

title: Add NaN/Inf gradient propagation tests for AD edge cases
description:
CONTEXT: The AD engine (fj-ad) has no tests verifying how NaN and Inf propagate through VJP/JVP rules. JAX propagates NaN faithfully: grad(sin)(NaN) = NaN, grad(exp)(Inf) = Inf. Silent NaN-to-zero or Inf-to-zero conversion in gradient rules would be a correctness bug that goes undetected.

SCOPE: Add targeted tests in crates/fj-ad/src/lib.rs verifying:
  1. NaN propagation: grad(sin)(NaN)=NaN, grad(cos)(NaN)=NaN, grad(exp)(NaN)=NaN, grad(log)(NaN)=NaN, grad(tanh)(NaN)=NaN, grad(x^2)(NaN)=NaN
  2. Inf propagation: grad(exp)(Inf)=Inf, grad(log)(0+)=Inf or -Inf, grad(x^2)(Inf)=Inf, grad(1/x)(0)=(-Inf or NaN)
  3. Mixed: grad(x*y) where x=NaN, y=finite
  4. Backward pass with NaN cotangent: vjp(sin, 1.0, NaN) should produce NaN gradient
  5. JVP with NaN tangent: jvp(sin, 1.0, NaN) should produce NaN output tangent

TESTING: Each test checks is_nan() or is_infinite() on the gradient output. Use descriptive assertion messages. Log each test case.

ACCEPTANCE: All NaN/Inf propagation tests pass. No gradient rule silently converts NaN to 0 or Inf to finite.
close_reason: Already covered by AD NaN/Inf propagation tests; verified in full workspace test run
